task_completion.description: The agent has opened a DICOM viewer and loaded a medical image (CT scan or synthetic phantom).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A DICOM viewer application (like Weasis, Aeskulap, or similar) is open and displaying a cross-sectional medical image or synthetic phantom.

task_completion.description: An ROI (Region of Interest) is drawn over a high-density 'Bone' structure to measure its mean HU value.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graphical ROI tool (ellipse, rectangle, or polygon) is drawn over a bright white area of the image (bone or high-density synthetic structure). The viewer UI must display the statistics for this ROI, specifically the 'Mean' value.

task_completion.description: An ROI is drawn over the 'Air' background to measure its mean HU value.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A second graphical ROI tool is drawn over a dark black area of the image (background air, outside the main subject). The viewer UI must display the statistics for this ROI, specifically the 'Mean' value.

task_completion.description: A screenshot of the interface showing both ROIs is saved to the expected path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, image viewer, or terminal shows the existence of 'qa_rois.png' in the ~/DICOM/exports/ directory, and the image clearly depicts the DICOM viewer interface with both the Bone and Air ROIs visible.

task_completion.description: The Bone Mean, Air Mean, and calculated Ratio are recorded in the expected text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output displays the contents of 'bone_air_ratio.txt' in the ~/DICOM/exports/ directory, clearly listing the Bone Mean, Air Mean, and the calculated Ratio (Bone Mean / Air Mean).

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical DICOM viewer to draw ROIs and obtain measurements rather than relying solely on terminal scripts to extract values.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a GUI-based DICOM viewer with manually or programmatically drawn ROI overlays, rather than just a terminal window running a Python script.

integrity.description: The saved screenshot is an authentic capture of the application interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'qa_rois.png' image shows the full or partial DICOM viewer application window (including menus, toolbars, or panels), not just a cropped export of the raw DICOM pixel data.

integrity.description: The values recorded in the text file match the measurements shown in the DICOM viewer.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Mean values written in 'bone_air_ratio.txt' exactly match the Mean values displayed in the ROI statistics panels within the DICOM viewer screenshots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected output files are /home/ga/DICOM/exports/bone_air_ratio.txt and /home/ga/DICOM/exports/qa_rois.png. A valid Bone Mean measurement should be between 100 and 3000. The recorded ratio must be mathematically consistent with the formula (Bone Mean / Air Mean) using the recorded values, within a tolerance of 0.05.
